q.en: Where does the pepper we eat come from?
q.zh: 我们吃的胡椒来自哪里？

options.en: A root that grows underground. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 长在地下的根。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A dried berry called a peppercorn that grows on a vine.
options.zh: 一种叫胡椒粒的干浆果，长在藤上。

options.en: A leaf picked from a tree.
options.zh: 从树上摘下的叶子。

options.en: Salt taken from the sea.
options.zh: 从海里取的盐。

explain.en: The Introduction says 'The pepper you eat comes from a dried-up berry called a peppercorn... It is a berry that grows on a vine.'
explain.zh: 引言说“你吃的胡椒来自一种叫胡椒粒的干浆果……它是长在藤上的浆果”。

q.en: Why is pepper called the King of Spices?
q.zh: 为什么胡椒被称为“香料之王”？

options.en: Because it is used more than any other spice in the world.
options.zh: 因为全世界它的使用量超过任何其他香料。

options.en: Because it is the heaviest spice.
options.zh: 因为它是最重的香料。

options.en: Because it is the most expensive spice now. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 因为它现在是是最贵的香料。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Because it grows only in one country.
options.zh: 因为它只长在一个国家。

explain.en: The book says 'Pepper is known as the King of Spices. This is because it is used more than any other spice in the world.'
explain.zh: 书中说“胡椒被称为香料之王。这是因为全世界它的使用量超过任何其他香料”。

q.en: How long did it take to get pepper from India to Europe in the 1400s?
q.zh: 在15世纪，把胡椒从印度运到欧洲需要多长时间？

options.en: One week
options.zh: 一个星期

options.en: A few hours
options.zh: 几个小时

options.en: Over a year
options.zh: 一年多

options.en: One day
options.zh: 一天

explain.en: The book states 'In the 1400s, it took over a year to get pepper from India to Europe' because there were no fast vehicles.
explain.zh: 书中说“在15世纪，把胡椒从印度运到欧洲需要一年多”，因为当时没有快速的交通工具。

q.en: What does the word 'grind' mean as used in the book?
q.zh: 书中使用的“grind”一词是什么意思？

options.en: To sail a boat.
options.zh: 驾船。

options.en: To plant a seed.
options.zh: 播种。

options.en: To crush something into little pieces or powder.
options.zh: 把某物碾碎成小块或粉末。

options.en: To cook food in water. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 用水煮食物。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines 'grind' as 'to crush something into little pieces or powder,' as people do to peppercorns.
explain.zh: 词汇表将“grind”定义为“把某物碾碎成小块或粉末”，正如人们对胡椒粒所做的那样。

q.en: Why does the author compare pepper to 'little bits of gold'?
q.zh: 作者为什么把胡椒比作“小金粒”？

options.en: To show pepper was once so valuable it was like treasure.
options.zh: 为了说明胡椒曾经非常珍贵，如同宝藏。

options.en: Because it sinks in water.
options.zh: 因为它会沉入水中。

options.en: Because it is yellow in color.
options.zh: 因为它是黄色的。

options.en: Because pepper is made of gold. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 因为胡椒是金子做的。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The ending says a shaker of pepper in the 1400s was 'like sprinkling your food with little bits of gold,' showing its great value then.
explain.zh: 结尾说在15世纪一整瓶胡椒“就像往食物上撒小金粒”，说明它当时的巨大价值。

q.en: How did supply and demand affect pepper's price long ago?
q.zh: 供求关系在很久以前如何影响胡椒的价格？

options.en: Price never changed.
options.zh: 价格从未改变。

options.en: Small supply and big demand let sellers charge a high price.
options.zh: 供给少、需求大，使卖家能要高价。

options.en: Big supply and small demand raised the price. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 供给多、需求小抬高了价格。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Free pepper was always expensive.
options.zh: 免费的胡椒总是很贵。

explain.en: The book says there was 'a very small supply of pepper but a big demand for it,' so people could sell it for a high price.
explain.zh: 书中说“胡椒供给很少但需求很大”，所以人们能卖很高的价钱。

q.en: How is pepper moved around the world today compared to the 1400s?
q.zh: 今天胡椒在世界各地的运输与15世纪相比有什么不同？

options.en: People only carry it by hand.
options.zh: 人们只用双手搬运。

options.en: Fast ships, trains, trucks, and airplanes deliver it in a few hours.
options.zh: 快速的轮船、火车、卡车和飞机能在几小时内送达。

options.en: It is never shipped at all.
options.zh: 它根本不被运输。

options.en: It still takes over a year by wagon and boat. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 仍然要靠马车和船花一年多时间。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says today people 'ship peppercorns around the world in just a few hours' using fast ships, trains, trucks, and airplanes.
explain.zh: 书中说今天人们用快速的轮船、火车、卡车和飞机“在几小时内把胡椒粒运到世界各地”。

q.en: What was the author's main purpose in writing this book?
q.zh: 作者写这本书的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To inform readers about pepper's history, growth, and value.
options.zh: 为了向读者介绍胡椒的历史、生长和珍贵价值。

options.en: To tell a make-believe fairy tale.
options.zh: 为了讲一个虚构的童话。

options.en: To convince readers to stop eating pepper. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了说服读者停止吃胡椒。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To teach how to cook a full meal.
options.zh: 为了教人做一顿完整的饭。

explain.en: The book is informational, sharing real facts about where pepper grows, its journey, and why it was once precious.
explain.zh: 这是一本知识性书籍，分享了关于胡椒生长地、运输历程，以及它曾经为何珍贵的事实。

q.en: How does the author make the long pepper journey easy to follow?
q.zh: 作者如何让漫长的胡椒运输历程易于理解？

options.en: By only using a map with no words. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 通过只用一张没有文字的地图。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: By starting at the end first.
options.zh: 通过先从结尾开始讲。

options.en: By skipping most of the trip.
options.zh: 通过略过大部分路程。

options.en: By describing each step from India to Europe in order.
options.zh: 通过按顺序描述从印度到欧洲的每一步。

explain.en: The 'Long, Expensive Journey' section follows the pepper step by step (picked, wagon, boat, Africa, Nile, Europe) so readers can track it.
explain.zh: “漫长而昂贵的旅程”一节按步骤追踪胡椒（采摘、马车、船、非洲、尼罗河、欧洲），方便读者理解。

q.en: How were peppercorns sometimes used as money?
q.zh: 胡椒粒曾如何被当作钱来用？

options.en: They were thrown away as trash.
options.zh: 它们被当垃圾扔掉。

options.en: They were only used to build houses. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 它们只被用来盖房子。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: They were eaten instead of coins.
options.zh: 它们被吃掉而不是当硬币。

options.en: People were paid in peppercorns and used them to buy things or pay rent.
options.zh: 人们用胡椒粒领工资，并用它们买东西或付房租。

explain.en: The book says peppercorns 'were sometimes used as money'—people got paid in them, bought needs, and even paid rent with them.
explain.zh: 书中说胡椒粒“有时被当作钱用”——人们用它领工资、买所需物品，甚至用它付房租。

prompt.en: The book asks: 'Do you think pepper will always be the King of Spices?' Give your opinion and one reason, using what you learned from the book.
prompt.zh: 书中提问：“你认为胡椒会一直是香料之王吗？”给出你的观点和一个理由，并运用你从书中学到的内容。

guidance.en:
- Accept either yes or no as long as it is supported.
- Students can note pepper is still used more than any other spice and tastes the same as 2000 years ago.
- Or they may argue new spices could become more popular.
guidance.zh:
- 只要理由充分，支持“是”或“否”均可。
- 学生可指出胡椒仍比任何其他香料用得多，且和2000年前味道一样。
- 也可争辩说新香料可能变得更受欢迎。
